title: Nursery Rhyme. CCVII. Riddles.
content:
[The man had one eye, and the tree two apples upon it.]

        There was a man who had no eyes,
        He went abroad to view the skies;
        He saw a tree with apples on it,
        He took no apples off, yet left no apples on it.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Nursery Rhyme. CCVII. Riddles.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
